_meta.description: Multi-day work-related side plots that add narrative texture to the simulation. Each active side plot spans 2-5 simulated days, involves 2-4 agents, and is logged to agents/reports/side-plots/. Triggered/advanced by agent-runner.js's scheduled() during the daily work-day cycle, narrated via meeting-engine.js's model calls when a side plot reaches a meeting-relevant stage.

rivalry_escalation.label: Rivalry Escalation
rivalry_escalation.trigger: relationships.json rivalries['architect-vs-lead-qa'] reaches stage_2 (2+ IRRITATED events from agent_10 caused by agent_8's audits within 30 days)

stages.event: Agent 8's audit report publicly questions one of Agent 10's release packages.
Agent 10 (IRRITATED) researches deeply per his behavioral_rules; tension visible in daily standups.
Public disagreement surfaces in the next weekly/monthly meeting — both agents present competing positions.
Optional: Team Lead (7) or CEO (11) mediates (Diplomat trait).
Resolution: Agent 10 returns with an improved version of the rejected suggestion, OR Agent 8's audit validates the release — rivalry tension reduced by one stage either way.

rivalry_escalation.resolution: Apply relationships.json rivalries['architect-vs-lead-qa'].resolution_paths; never resolves via Agent 10 bypassing authority.

pip_drama.label: PIP Drama
agents: optional: 1 (Perfectionist) or 6 (QA) as a sympathetic mentor
pip_drama.trigger: Team Lead (7) places a regular agent (1-4) on a PIP via promotion-config.json's pip criteria failures (see relationships.json pip_session)

stages.event: PIP session held (private_coaching/pip_session meeting type); public metrics updated, private coaching begins.
Agent under PIP shows visible mood dip (IRRITATED or low mood); a mentor agent (1 or 6) may informally check in.
Mid-PIP checkpoint: small win or further setback, narrated via Gemini based on the agent's recent case outcomes.
Team Lead (7) reviews progress against the PIP's measurable targets.
Resolution: PIP graduated (mood/metrics recover) or extended for another simulated month — recorded in `promotions` table (track='pip').

pip_drama.resolution: Outcome recorded via meeting-engine.js pip_session decisions.config_overrides and the `promotions` table.

breakthrough.label: Breakthrough
agents: optional: agent_5 or agent_10 as a recognizing senior
breakthrough.trigger: An agent solves an 'advanced' difficulty case cleanly (HAPPY/COMPLACENT transition) on the first attempt, with a high app-response quality score (> 0.85)

stages.event: The breakthrough case is solved; agent enters HAPPY (and possibly COMPLACENT if agent_5/agent_10).
Recognition: a senior agent (5 or 10) or the Team Lead (7) acknowledges the breakthrough in the next standup, providing a team-wide morale boost.
Optional follow-on: the agent fileSuggestion()s a write-up of the approach based on the breakthrough.

breakthrough.resolution: Mood boost applied to the breakthrough agent and a smaller boost to attendees of the recognizing standup/meeting.

bonus_day_drama.label: Bonus Day Drama
bonus_day_drama.trigger: Agent 2 (The Productive)'s bonus_day_trigger fires (weekly productivity +30% above target)

stages.event: Bonus day announced/scheduled; Agent 2 plans an extended optimization session.
Bonus day session: Agent 2 (and any collaborators) focus on Claude/app optimization suggestions, fileSuggestion()-ing several improvements.
Resolution: suggestions reviewed at the next weekly meeting; if multiple are approved, this can chain into a 'breakthrough' side plot for Agent 2.

bonus_day_drama.resolution: fileSuggestion() outputs reviewed in the weekly meeting's suggestion_decisions.

inspiration_event.label: Inspiration Event
agents: optional: 11 (CEO) or 10 (Architect) as the source of inspiration
inspiration_event.trigger: Agent 9 (The Designer)'s INSPIRED state triggers (UI fondness >= inspired_threshold of 51%)

stages.event: Designer enters INSPIRED — artistic_boost_when_inspired (3.00x) applies; an 'inspired token' is issued.
Designer produces an early UI-flag report at an accelerated pace.
Optional: a senior agent's positive feedback (CEO's mood_boost_frequency or Architect's vision trait) extends the inspired streak.
Resolution: Designer's happiness level resets to the current UI fondness value (per agent_9.states.INSPIRED.effects), ending the event.

inspiration_event.resolution: agent_9 happiness reset to current fondness; any shipped UI changes logged.

comparison_event.label: Comparison Event
agents: optional: 6 (QA) if the comparison feeds an audit
comparison_event.trigger: crm-engine.js recordCompareAlternatives() logs a 'compare_alternatives' interaction for an agent (typically Agent 2's FOUND-OUTSIDE PATTERN)

stages.event: Agent compares Claude's answer to an externally-found answer; outcome (claude_better/external_better) recorded.
If external_better: agent fileSuggestion()s a 'mock report' showing how easy the external answer was. If claude_better: agent's confidence in the app increases.

Resolution: getModelUsageAdjustment() may nudge the agent's effective model_usage_rate (+/-0.05) based on the rolling win rate; QA (6) may pick up a strong external_better streak as an audit_session topic.

comparison_event.resolution: model_usage_rate delta applied via configOverrides; optionally seeds the next audit_session's agenda.

meeting_tension.label: Meeting Tension
meeting_tension.trigger: Any meeting (meeting-engine.js runMeeting) where decisions.irritation_effects includes 2+ agents, OR a rivalry pair (relationships.json rivalries) are both attendees

stages.event: Tense meeting occurs; irritation_effects applied to 2+ attendees.
Lingering tension affects the next day's standup — affected agents' personality triggers fire more readily (compounding effect, especially if Lead QA agent_8's meeting_trigger_amplifier was active).
Resolution: a follow-up 1:1 (private_coaching) or a positive HAPPY event for one of the affected agents defuses the tension.

meeting_tension.resolution: Irritation decays via normal resolveIrritation()/resetWeeklyState() flow, or is resolved early by a HAPPY event for an affected agent.

lifecycle.tracking: Active side plots are persisted in the `side_plots` table (schema.sql Part 10): id, type, agents (JSON array), start_day, duration_days, current_stage, status ('active'|'resolved'), report_path.
lifecycle.advance: Each simulated work day, agent-runner.js's scheduled() advances `current_stage` for all 'active' side plots whose stage list has an entry for that day, narrates the stage via meeting-engine.js-style Gemini calls when the stage involves dialogue, and applies the stage's effects.
lifecycle.resolve: When current_stage reaches the final stage, status -> 'resolved', the markdown report is finalized and committed to output_path, and the row remains in `side_plots` for year-tracker.json's stats.
